name: Castillo de Cabrera and Punta de n'Ensiola lighthouse — circular route through the Cabrera Archipelago National Park
name_original: Castillo de Cabrera y faro de Punta de n'Ensiola — circular por el Parque Nacional del Archipiélago de Cabrera
region_intro: Hiking around l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera offers exploration of a maritime-terrestrial national park characterized by dry, hilly terrain and a rugged coastline. The landscape features numerous coves, dramatic cliffs, and secluded beaches. Inland, the area is dominated by thick scrubland and Aleppo pines, providing habitat for diverse wildlife. The main island, Illa de Cabrera, presents a blend of coastal and terrestrial features for hikers. Best easy hiking trails around l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera The most popular easy hiking route is Sa Platgeta Beach – View of the Cabrera Lighthouse loop from Cabrera, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete, offering views towards the historic lighthouse. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabrera Castle – Blue Cave (Sa Cova Blava) loop from Cabrera, an easy 1.9 miles (3.0 km) path. This route includes a visit to the 14th-century Cabrera Castle, providing panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Cabrera Castle – Sa Platgeta Beach loop from Cabrera, a 2.3 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through coastal scrubland and past historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour. Hiking around l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera is defined by rugged coastlines, scrubland, and historical landmarks like Cabrera Castle. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails. The routes in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1,800 hikers have used komoot to explore l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera?
A: There are 12 easy hiking trails in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera, out of a total of 18 routes. These trails are generally well-suited for beginners and those looking for a relaxed walk.

Q: Do I need a permit to hike on Illa de Cabrera?
A: Yes, access to Illa de Cabrera, the main island, is limited and requires a permit. As a maritime-terrestrial national park, visitor numbers are controlled to preserve its unspoiled character. You should arrange your permit and boat trip in advance.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera?
A: Easy hikes in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera feature dry, hilly terrain with rugged coastlines. You'll encounter scrubland, Aleppo pines, and paths leading to coves and secluded beaches. The trails are generally well-defined but can be uneven in places.

Q: Are there any circular easy hiking routes in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera?
A: Yes, several easy circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31433734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Platgeta Beach – View of the Cabrera Lighthouse loop from Cabrera&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5301218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabrera Castle – Sa Platgeta Beach loop from Cabrera&lt;/a&gt;, both offering diverse scenery.

Q: What notable landmarks or natural features can I see on easy hikes?
A: Easy hikes often lead to significant points of interest. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/194564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabrera Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views, or explore paths near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/330276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de n'Ensiola Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass by beautiful spots like Sa Platgeta Beach and offer views towards the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/194567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Cave (Sa Cova Blava)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the easy trails in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are suitable for families, offering manageable distances and relatively low elevation changes. Routes that include a visit to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/194564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabrera Castle&lt;/a&gt; or lead to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5301218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Platgeta Beach&lt;/a&gt; are often enjoyed by families.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera?
A: The best time for easy hikes is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making longer hikes less comfortable, while winters are generally mild but can be windy.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cabrera Archipelago National Park?
A: No, dogs are generally not permitted in Cabrera Archipelago Maritime-Terrestrial National Park to protect the delicate ecosystem and wildlife, which includes endemic species like the Balearic lizard and numerous bird species. It's best to leave pets at home when visiting.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera?
A: The easy trails in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning coastal views, the historical landmarks like Cabrera Castle, and the clear, turquoise waters visible from the paths.

Q: How do I get to the starting points for hikes on Illa de Cabrera?
A: Access to Illa de Cabrera is primarily by boat from Mallorca, typically from Port de Sant Jordi or Colònia de Sant Jordi. These boat trips are often part of organized tours that include transport to the island and sometimes guided walks. You'll need to arrange your boat trip in advance, especially during peak season.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, the clear, turquoise waters around Cabrera are ideal for swimming. Many easy trails lead to secluded beaches like Sa Platgeta, which is perfect for a refreshing dip. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/194567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Cave (Sa Cova Blava)&lt;/a&gt;, while typically accessed by boat, is also a popular spot for swimming in its vibrant blue waters.

Q: What should I pack for an easy hike in l'Arxipèlag de Cabrera?
A: Given the dry, hilly terrain and coastal environment, it's advisable to pack plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), sturdy walking shoes, and a light jacket for potential sea breezes. Don't forget your swimwear if you plan to enjoy the beaches.
tips: The tower of the fort is mountable and from here you can enjoy great views over the island and the bay to the port drive.

I have rarely seen such great and crystal clear water.
A swim stop is worthwhile here.

Lighthouse located in the southwest of the Island of Cabrera, in the Cabrera Archipelago National Park in the Balearic Islands.

Although work on the lighthouse finished in 1868, the optical apparatus was installed in 1870. The original project of August 1861 was a 2nd order lighthouse with a large circular building about 25 m in diameter and with the tower not in the center but in the internal part of the arc of circumference facing the sea.
